program.name: Early Head Start
program.description:
Early childhood learning and family support for infants, toddlers, and pregnant women in Texas. Early Head Start provides free early learning, health care, healthy meals, and family support. Families also get help with parenting and community resources.

Early Head Start is run by local agencies, so services and availability vary by location. There may be a waitlist in some areas.

Some programs can also enroll families with income between 100% and 130% of the poverty level if they have open slots. A small number of spots (up to 10%) may go to any child who could benefit, even if the family does not meet the income or other requirements.

Children in foster care, those experiencing homelessness, and families receiving TANF, SNAP, or SSI are eligible regardless of income.

When programs have limited slots, priority is given to the lowest-income families, children with disabilities, and families experiencing homelessness.
program.description_short: Free early learning program for infants and toddlers
program.estimated_value: Varies - free preschool, meals, and health services
program.website_description: Free early learning and support for infants, toddlers, and pregnant women

documents.text: Proof of home address (ex: lease, utility bill)
Proof of identity for child and parent
Proof of income (ex: pay stubs, tax returns, benefit letters)
Child's birth certificate or proof of age
Proof of TANF, SNAP, or SSI benefits (if applicable)
